Nazi Looted Booksat Penn Libraries
Identify a bookHow they were foundBrowse the booksSources & lists
About ▾
ScopeThe methodThe five stepsWhat we cannot account forWhere to look
/markings/anatomy/

Where to look

Reference volumes show you an idealised stamp. They never show you where it hides. Select a surface of the book.

The book as a sequence of surfaces

Reference plates show you an idealised stamp. They never show you where it hides. Select a surface — the evidence is rarely on the page you would open to first.

Where to look

Inside front board

front pastedown
in-situ photograph
Inside front board
Recorded here in 512 books
JCR bookplate
Jewish Cultural Reconstruction, Inc.
Johannes Pohl pastedown
handwritten Hebrew transliteration, ERR
Nazi stamp
often lies beneath a later bookplate
Look through — or under — a later bookplate. The earliest evidence is usually the layer nobody removed.
On a phone

The fanned diagram needs width. On narrow screens it collapses to a vertical list of surfaces — tap one to expand its markings inline — keeping the same order, so the “front board → spine” sequence still reads as the physical book.

Provenance research & text: Bruce Nielsen · Penn Libraries, Katz Center for Advanced Judaic StudiesUncertainty in this record is shown, not hidden.
